content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcel PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
Jloly 5,2002
Robert Dresser, Chief Counsel
Department of Information Technology
801 K Street, Suite 2100
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-022
Dear Mr. Dresser:
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistancel on behalf of the
Department of Information Technology ("DOIT") regarding the financial disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").2
The question you pose in your request is how "serving in a staff capacity" has
been construed by the Commission. This question arose in the course of reviewing
certain project responsibilities in connection with a contract to assist DOIT in updating
the Statewide IT Strategic Plan and DoIT's procedures relating to the "Agency
Information Management Strategy" reports.
In responding to your request, first, we apologize for the delay in providing a
response. While we genuinely try to respond to all requests for advice in a timely
manner, we were unable to respond prior to DOIT's unexpected termination at the end of
Ivne 2002. We apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused you or your
department.
In reviewing your letter and all of the supporting documents you provided, it
appears that the question in part involves past conduct, i.e. conduct which had already
occurred at the time we received your letter. Pursuant to regulation 18730(b)(5)(B), all
persons assuming designated positions are required to file Statements of Economic
Interests within 30 days of assuming their designated positions. As it appears that the
I Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formalwrittenadvice(GovernmentCode$83114;2Cal.CodeofRegs.gl8329(c)(3),copyenclosed.)
2 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission rJgulations iiiit xiitle Z, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
',J6+;


File No. I-02-022
Page No. 2
contract about which you inquired was originally entered into on December 19,200l,the
contractor employees would have been required to file their assuming office statements
no later than January 18,2002.
However, general guidance on issues involving disclosure may be provided to
persons who have a duty to advise other persons relating to their duties ,nd". the Act.
(Regulation 18329(c)(l).) The general question you pose in your request is whether
certain contract employees would be "serving in a staff cap acity" rrrder the provisions of
regulation 18701.
Regulation 1 8701 (a) states:
"(2) 'Consultant' means an individual who, pursuant to a
contract with a state or local government agency:
fl. . .11
"(B) Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that
capacity participates in making a govemmental decision as
defined in Regulation 18702.2 or performs the same or
substantially all the same duties for the agency that would
otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position
specified in the agency's Conflict of Interest Code under
Government Code Secti on 87 302.,, (emphasis added.)
The concept of "serving in a staff capacity" has also been construed by the
Commission staff in advice letters to have a quantitative and a temporal quality, in that
persons who perform limited projects for an agency over a limited periodof time are
generally not deemed to be serving in a staff capacity. (See Randoiph Advice Letter,
No. I-95-045; Conley Advice Letter, No. 4-96-182; Marta AdviceLetter, No. 4-98-073;
Smith Advice Lettel No. I-99-316.) Implicit in the notion of service in a staff capacity is
an ongoing relationship between the contractor and the public agency. (Sanchez Advice
Letter,No. A-97-438.)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Holly B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question: How has 'serving in a staff capacity' been construed by the Commission? This question arose in the course of reviewing certain project responsibilities in connection with a contract to assist DOIT in updating the Statewide IT Strategic Plan and DoIT's procedures relating to the 'Agency Information Management Strategy' reports.
sections.conclusion: The concept of 'serving in a staff capacity' has been construed by the Commission staff in advice letters to have a quantitative and a temporal quality, in that persons who perform limited projects for an agency over a limited period of time are generally not deemed to be serving in a staff capacity. Implicit in the notion of service in a staff capacity is an ongoing relationship between the contractor and the public agency.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether contract employees working on specific IT projects for the Department of Information Technology would be considered as 'serving in a staff capacity' under Political Reform Act disclosure requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Employees performing limited projects for an agency over a limited time period are generally not deemed to be serving in a staff capacity; serving in a staff capacity implies an ongoing relationship between the contractor and the public agency.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document discusses past conduct and cites multiple prior advice letters as precedent. Letter notes delay in response due to DOIT's unexpected termination in June 2002.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How has 'serving in a staff capacity' been construed by the Commission? This question arose in the course of reviewing certain project responsibilities in connection with a contract to assist DOIT in updating the Statewide IT Strategic Plan and DoIT's procedures relating to the 'Agency Information Management Strategy' reports.

CONCLUSION: The concept of 'serving in a staff capacity' has been construed by the Commission staff in advice letters to have a quantitative and a temporal quality, in that persons who perform limited projects for an agency over a limited period of time are generally not deemed to be serving in a staff capacity. Implicit in the notion of service in a staff capacity is an ongoing relationship between the contractor and the public agency.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the Department of Information Technology's request for informal assistance regarding whether certain contract employees working on IT strategic planning projects would be required to file financial disclosures as persons 'serving in a staff capacity' under the Political Reform Act.
